topic: n8n AI Workflow Tutorial 2026: Build Your First AI-Powered Automation

competitors.title: Building AI Agent Workflows in n8n: The 2026 Complete Guide
key_points: Detailed breakdown of n8n AI Agent node architecture: Brain (LLM connection), Memory (context management), Tools (agent capabilities), System Prompt (agent personality)
Supported LLM providers: OpenAI GPT-4/GPT-4 Turbo, Anthropic Claude 3 Opus/Sonnet/Haiku, Azure OpenAI, Ollama (local models), Google Gemini
Memory types: Window Memory (last N messages), Buffer Memory (full conversation), Vector Store Memory (semantic search over history)
Built-in tools: HTTP Request, Code (JS/Python), Calculator, Wikipedia, Custom Tools via sub-workflows
Advanced patterns: Multi-tool agents, Agent Chains (Research → Analysis → Writer), RAG Integration
Cost optimization: Token monitoring, caching strategies (Redis + n8n built-in), model selection (GPT-4 for complex reasoning, GPT-3.5/Haiku for simple tasks)
Real-world use cases: Customer support triage, content research, data transformation, monitoring & alerting

competitors.title: n8n AI Automation Workflows: Step-by-Step Tutorial (2026 Guide)
key_points: Complete 7-step tutorial: Install n8n → Understand workflow structure → Create first AI workflow → Test → Deploy → Advanced patterns → Management
Two setup options: n8n Cloud (beginner) or Self-Hosted via Docker/npm/VPS (advanced, more control and lower cost)
Workflow structure: Trigger → Processing Logic → Output Action
Practical example: AI Email Auto-Responder with trigger, AI model node, logic control (IF/Switch), and response sending
Advanced patterns: Multi-step AI processing, AI + database integration (Airtable/Notion/PostgreSQL/Google Sheets), RAG setup
Management best practices: Version control, execution log monitoring, error handling with Error Trigger Node + retry logic + fallback responses
Full AI Lead Qualification Workflow blueprint: Trigger → Data Cleaning → AI Analysis → Conditional Logic → CRM/Slack/Email routing

competitors.title: n8n AI Automation: Complete Guide to Building AI Workflows (2026)
key_points: n8n advantages for AI: no per-operation costs (self-hosted), full data privacy, most advanced AI-native features of any workflow platform
AI Node ecosystem: AI Agent node, LangChain nodes, Chat model nodes, Tool nodes, Memory nodes, Vector store nodes (Pinecone/Qdrant/Supabase/Chroma)
Real-world example: Research agent that monitors competitors daily, costs ~$0.50-1.00 per run (vs $50+/month on Make/Zapier for same volume)
Setup options: n8n Cloud ($20/month), Self-hosted VPS ($5-10/month), Railway/Render/Fly.io (one-click deploys with free tiers)
Pros: Free self-hosted, advanced AI nodes, data privacy, active community, unlimited workflows
Cons: Steeper learning curve than Zapier, self-hosting requires server management, fewer native integrations, AI nodes still evolving

competitors.title: n8n or Zapier: Who's winning workflow automation right now? (2026 data)
key_points: n8n grew mid-market customer count 10x year-over-year (Jan 2025 to Jan 2026), from 12 to 122 customers
Customer count grew 14% month-over-month from Dec 2025 to Jan 2026
Nearly 80% of n8n's new customers were already Zapier users — n8n is gaining traction within Zapier's base
16% of all customers using either n8n or Zapier now use both tools
Among joint users, customers are 50% more likely to drop Zapier than n8n (n8n winning replacement battle 2:1)
n8n raised $60M Series B (Mar 2025) and $180M Series C (Oct 2025)
Higher-spend mid-market adoption ($10K+ annually) plateaued at ~10% of base since Oct 2025
Enterprise adoption limited — customer count flat for past 4 months as of Apr 2026
